name: gate-duplicate-hook
description: The contract declares the SAME export name (`useCheckout`) under two different packages — `@stripe/ggui-checkout` and `@paypal/ggui-checkout` — and the app registers BOTH `(package, export)` pairs, so registry membership and public-env are clean; the only violation is the cross-package name collision. The boilerplate emits one `import { useCheckout }` per export name, so same-named exports collide in module scope regardless of package (SPEC §7.9). The gate MUST reject with `duplicate_gadget_hook`. Note the wire is package-keyed, so a name cannot repeat WITHIN one package; the hazard is strictly cross-package. Because every pair here resolves cleanly, this case pins NO priority between the duplicate check and the registry/env gates — a conformant implementation may run the checks in any order.
